texts.label: text
texts.orig: Abstract In this paper, we investigate the global existence of almost surely positive solution to a stochastic Nicholson's blowflies delay differential equation with regime switching, and give the estimation of the path. The results presented in this paper extend some corresponding results in Wang et al. Stochastic Nicholson's blowflies delayed differential equations, Appl. Math. Lett. 87 (2019) 20-26 .
texts.text: Abstract In this paper, we investigate the global existence of almost surely positive solution to a stochastic Nicholson's blowflies delay differential equation with regime switching, and give the estimation of the path. The results presented in this paper extend some corresponding results in Wang et al. Stochastic Nicholson's blowflies delayed differential equations, Appl. Math. Lett. 87 (2019) 20-26 .

texts.orig: 4 Conclusion
texts.text: 4 Conclusion

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In this paper, we consider a stochastic Nicholson's blowflies delay differential equation with regime switching. To the best of our knowledge it is the first time that regime switching is considered in Nicholson's blowflies model. By choosing several suitable V functions we prove that the solution X ( t ) of the stochastic Nicholson's blowflies model is globally existent, which is also positive on R + 0 almost surely, and present some estimation of the solution X ( t ) to model (1.2)-(1.3). The results obtained in this paper extend some corresponding results in literature, especially we removed the restricted condition δ > σ 2 / 2, which is needed in [3]. texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1) Find conditions for the almost surely strong (weak) persistence of the population, that is, under what conditions lim inf t →∞ X ( t ) > 0 a.s. (lim sup t →∞ X ( t ) > 0 a.s.); or more accurately, estimate the maximum value of K ∈ R + such that lim inf t →∞ X ( t ) ⩾ K a.s.
texts.text: Find conditions for the almost surely strong (weak) persistence of the population, that is, under what conditions lim inf t →∞ X ( t ) > 0 a.s. (lim sup t →∞ X ( t ) > 0 a.s.); or more accurately, estimate the maximum value of K ∈ R + such that lim inf t →∞ X ( t ) ⩾ K a.s.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2) In the case of p > 0, τ > 0, the delay τ and parameter a vary along the Markov chain ( r t ) t ⩾ 0 , that is, they depend on the Markov chain ( r t ) t ⩾ 0 . Find conditions for the upper boundedness of the population, that is, under what conditions there exists a M ∈ R + such that lim sup t →∞ X ( t ) ⩽ M a.s., and give the estimation of this upper bound M . Find conditions for the negativeness of the Lyapunov exponent of X ( t ), that is lim sup t →∞ 1 t log X ( t ) < 0 a.s.
